_readme: Vanity-URL resolver: maps a public handle to the immutable uid of a venue
or artist, for URLs like tonight.quest/{handle}. Hand-maintained for now —
there is no self-serve claiming yet; a handle is assigned by editing this
file. Squares are NOT resolved here: they live under their own /sq/{name}
namespace, so square names never collide with venue/artist handles and are
IDENTITY vs HANDLE. A uid is the frozen internal key that events and every
relation join on (venue uid = the key in data/venues.json, e.g.
'lamplighter-broadway'; artist uid = the 'id' in data/artists.json). It never
changes. A handle is the mutable public slug that appears in the URL. When a
venue/artist wants a different URL, add the NEW handle as canonical and keep
the OLD one with canonical:false so existing links redirect instead of 404 —
never repoint or delete a uid, only ever add/retire handles.
RESOLUTION. Front end lowercases the path segment, looks it up in 'handles'.
If found and canonical, render the target; if found and not canonical,
redirect to that uid's canonical handle. A handle in 'reserved' (or matching
an existing app route/page) can never be assigned.

_fields.handles: map of handle (lowercase) -> { type: 'venue'|'artist', uid, canonical }. Exactly one canonical handle per uid; extra entries with canonical:false are retained old handles that redirect to it.
_fields.reserved: handles that can never be assigned — app routes, page names, and namespace prefixes. Square names are intentionally absent (they live under /sq/).
